model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: This looks like a true coin-flip between two nearly identical top-five teams, but Houston gets the slightest edge because its defense is marginally better in both KenPom and Torvik and that gives it a sturdier floor in a high-leverage Elite Eight game. Florida absolutely has a credible path and may be a touch better offensively, so this is not a strong fade of the Gators. Still, with Houston’s balance, defensive reliability, and proven ability to win grind-it-out games, I’d lean narrowly to the Cougars to reach the Final Four.

reasoningStep.title: Balanced top-five matchup leans Houston by a hair
reasoningStep.summary: Florida and Houston are almost dead even in the predictive metrics, with Florida slightly better offensively and Houston slightly better defensively. No major availability issue surfaced to materially change the matchup, so the deciding factor is Houston’s marginal defensive edge and lower-variance profile in a game likely decided by execution.
evidence: KenPom: Florida No. 4 (AdjEM 33.83, AdjO 125.6, AdjD 91.8) vs Houston No. 5 (AdjEM 33.55, AdjO 124.6, AdjD 91.0).
Torvik: Florida No. 4 (AdjOE 126.12, AdjDE 92.21) vs Houston No. 5 (AdjOE 125.10, AdjDE 91.85).
Both teams are elite on both ends, but Houston holds the slightly better defensive rank in both systems while Florida holds the slightly better offensive rank.
Recent search results did not surface a confirmed major injury or availability concern significant enough to swing the pick.
Recent head-to-head context exists, with Florida beating Houston 65-63 in the 2025 title game, which reinforces how thin the margin is rather than creating a decisive edge.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: 2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: e8-south-1
Current game label: South Regional Final
Round: Elite 8
Resolved teams: Florida (florida) vs Houston (houston)
Prior committed picks: 62
